title: Static Frontend Hosting on Netlify
description: Host static frontends and JAMstack sites on Netlify with Git-driven deployments, serverless functions, form handling, and identity management built in. Deploy previews per branch enable frontend review workflows without separate infrastructure.

cost.costNotes: Free tier: 100GB bandwidth/month, 300 build minutes, unlimited sites, serverless functions (125K requests/month). Ideal for JAMstack/static sites; Pro plan ($19/month) for increased limits.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: This pattern explicitly targets Netlify's free tier, which provides 100GB bandwidth/month, 300 build minutes/month, 125K serverless function requests/month, and unlimited sites at $0/month. The min=0 represents staying within free tier limits (suitable for MVPs, personal projects, and low-traffic sites with <100K monthly visitors). The max=0 reflects that this pattern is specifically designed to remain free-tier eligible. If usage exceeds free tier limits, users would transition to Netlify Pro ($19/month) or Business ($99/month), but that would represent a different pattern (cost-paid-saas-tier). This pattern is only applicable when traffic, builds, and function calls remain within free tier boundaries.
provenance.adoptionCost: Very low adoption cost due to Netlify's streamlined deployment model. Implementation involves: (1) Structuring application as JAMstack architecture (1-3 hours for developers familiar with static site generators), (2) Configuring Git-based deployment via netlify.toml or UI (30 minutes), (3) Setting up serverless functions if needed (1-2 hours for basic CRUD operations), (4) Configuring forms/identity if needed (1 hour). Total: 4-7 hours at $50/hour blended rate with AI coding assistants = $200-$350. AI tools like GitHub Copilot significantly reduce boilerplate for serverless functions and configuration. The pattern is well-documented with abundant examples, making it accessible even to junior developers. No infrastructure provisioning, Docker setup, or complex CI/CD configuration required.
provenance.licenseCost: Netlify's free tier requires no license fees or upfront payments. The service operates on a freemium SaaS model where the free tier is permanently available (not a trial). No commercial licenses, enterprise agreements, or per-seat costs. Organizations can use the free tier indefinitely as long as usage remains within limits. The only potential cost would be optional add-ons (analytics, background functions) or exceeding free tier limits, but the core hosting, functions, forms, and identity features are genuinely free. This represents true $0 licensing cost for the pattern as designed.

provides.reasoning: Netlify's core offering is hosting static sites built with JAMstack, making this a primary capability of the pattern.
Netlify Functions (AWS Lambda wrapper) allows backend logic execution without server management, essential for JAMstack backends.
Netlify includes global CDN for content delivery with the free tier's 100GB bandwidth, core to its hosting architecture.
Netlify Forms provides built-in form submission handling without backend code, allowing JAMstack sites to capture user input and integrate with third-party services.
Netlify Identity provides authentication and user management features including sign-ups, logins, password recovery, and user database management on the free tier.
The 300 build minutes allocation provides CI/CD pipeline capability for automated deployments triggered by Git commits.
Netlify's deployment model is fundamentally Git-based with GitHub/GitLab integration, enabling continuous deployment from repository changes.

requires.reasoning: JAMstack architecture requires a tool to build static HTML/CSS/JS (e.g., Next.js, Hugo, Gatsby) before deployment to Netlify.
Netlify's continuous deployment model requires source code to be in a Git repository for automatic builds and deployments.
JAMstack prototypes/MVPs typically need React/Vue/Svelte or similar framework to build the static site content and interactive features.

supports_nfr.reason: Free tier lacks enterprise SLA, multi-region redundancy, and priority support needed for high availability targets.
Free-tier shared infrastructure and serverless function cold starts introduce minimum p95 latency of 100ms.
Free-tier resource contention and cold start variability result in p99 latency floor of 200ms.
Instant rollback to previous deployments via dashboard or API enables RTO as low as 1 minute.
Free tier bandwidth limit of 100GB/month and function execution limits cap peak throughput to 36000 jobs/hour.
Free tier bandwidth and serverless function concurrency limits constrain read QPS to 100 maximum.
Free tier function execution limits and form submission quotas cap write QPS at 50 maximum.
Netlify does not offer a HIPAA BAA on standard plans; not suitable for applications processing PHI.

supports_constraints.reason: Netlify is a cloud-agnostic SaaS platform that abstracts underlying infrastructure from users.
Netlify supports static site generators and serverless functions in multiple languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go.
Netlify is specifically designed for web hosting and JAMstack applications with API serverless functions.
Pattern specifically requires Netlify as the hosting provider
Netlify CDN provides built-in edge caching, but pattern works with or without application-level caching.
Multi-tenancy is supported at small scale with external free-tier databases, but not a pattern requirement.
Netlify serverless functions have execution time limits (10s free tier) that preclude batch processing workloads.
Netlify serverless architecture and free tier limitations do not support complex distributed transaction coordination.
Netlify free tier does not include WebSocket or real-time streaming capabilities for live data.
Free tiers of external vector databases (Pinecone, Supabase) can be integrated for limited vector search in prototypes.
Netlify does not provide database services; document storage requires external services outside this pattern.
Netlify does not provide persistent key-value storage; requires external services like Redis Cloud.
Netlify does not provide database services; graph database requirements need external providers.
Netlify is not designed for time-series data storage; requires external time-series database services.
Free-tier external databases can support OLTP workloads at small scale, but pattern works for both OLTP and non-OLTP.
Netlify is not designed for analytical processing workloads; optimized for static sites and lightweight serverless functions.

hosting_provider.description: JAMstack hosting provider. Netlify offers 100GB bandwidth and forms/identity. Vercel has better Next.js integration. Cloudflare Pages offers unlimited bandwidth but fewer built-in features.

function_runtime.description: Serverless function runtime. Node.js has broader ecosystem and faster cold starts. Go offers better performance for compute-intensive tasks.

enable_forms.description: Enable Netlify Forms for contact/submission forms without backend code. Free tier includes 100 submissions/month.

enable_identity.description: Enable Netlify Identity for user authentication and gated content. Free tier includes 1,000 active users. Adds complexity but eliminates need for separate auth service.

enable_split_testing.description: Enable A/B testing by splitting traffic between git branches. Useful for testing features but consumes bandwidth quota faster.

warn_nfr.reason: Free tier bandwidth limit of 100GB/month can be exhausted quickly at sustained high QPS, leading to service interruption.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-static-frontend--netlify: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>50). Free tier 100GB/month bandwidth may be exhausted quickly, causing service interruption mid-month.

warn_nfr.reason: Free tier serverless function execution limits and form submission quotas can be exhausted at sustained high write QPS.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-static-frontend--netlify: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (>25). Free tier function execution limits may be exhausted, causing throttling or service interruption.
